mission.title: Install and Configure Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)
mission.description: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment, management, and scaling of containerized applications using Kubernetes. It is ideal for users looking for a fully managed solution with integrated Azure services, but it may incur additional costs compared to self-managed Kubernetes clusters.

steps.title: Install Azure CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Azure CLI, which is required to interact with Azure resources.

```bash

curl -sL https://aka.ms/InstallAzureCLIDeb | sudo bash

brew update && brew install azure-cli

```

> For Debian-based systems.
> For macOS users.

steps.title: Create an AKS Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new AKS cluster with the specified Kubernetes version, node count, and region.

```bash

az aks create --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --node-count 3 --kubernetes-version 1.31 --location eastus

```

> Replace 'myResourceGroup' and 'myAKSCluster' with your desired names.
commands: az aks create --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --node-count 3 --kubernetes-version 1.31 --location eastus

steps.title: Configure kubectl
steps.description:
This command configures kubectl to use the new AKS cluster by updating the kubeconfig file.

```bash

az aks get-credentials --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster

```

> This allows kubectl to communicate with your AKS cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in the AKS cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up monitoring and autoscaling for your AKS cluster.

```bash

az aks enable-addons --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --addons monitoring

az aks update --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --enable-cluster-autoscaler --min-count 1 --max-count 5

```

> Enables monitoring via Azure Monitor.
> Enables cluster autoscaling.
commands: az aks enable-addons --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --addons monitoring

uninstall.title: Delete AKS Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the AKS cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs.

```bash

az aks delete --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --yes --no-wait

```

uninstall.title: Remove Azure CLI
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Azure CLI from your system if it is no longer needed.

```bash

sudo apt-get remove azure-cli

brew uninstall azure-cli

```

> For Debian-based systems.
> For macOS users.

upgrade.title: Upgrade AKS Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This command upgrades the AKS cluster to the specified version.

```bash

az aks get-upgrades --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster

az aks upgrade --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --kubernetes-version 1.31

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check available upgrades.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Verify that the nodes are updated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to get credentials for AKS cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified resource group or cluster name may be incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the resource group and cluster name using 'az aks list'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient permissions to create AKS cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Azure account does not have the necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure the account has 'Owner' or 'Contributor' role on the subscription.

troubleshooting.title: Nodes are in 'NotReady' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The nodes may not have enough resources or may be misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the node status using 'kubectl describe nodes' and adjust resource limits.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** kubectl is not installed or not in the PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl using 'az aks install-cli'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Azure regions and improved integration with Azure Monitor.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.26.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.30, ensure all workloads are compatible with the new version.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: az aks create --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --node-count 3 --kubernetes-version 1.31 --location eastus
az aks enable-addons --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --addons monitoring

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Azure CLI and kubectl installed before proceeding.
